questions.prompt: What is the capital of the United States?
questions.explanation: The capital of the United States is Washington, D.C., a federal district distinct from the states. New York, the largest city and home to the UN headquarters, is the usual trap because many believe it is the capital.

questions.prompt: Which is the most populated continent in the world?
questions.explanation: Asia is the most populated continent: it is home to more than half of humanity, including China and India, the two most populated countries. It is also the largest of the continents.

questions.prompt: What is the capital of Spain?
questions.explanation: Madrid, located in the centre of the country, is the capital of Spain and its largest city. Barcelona, the capital of Catalonia and a famous coastal metropolis, is the most tempting distractor.

questions.prompt: Across which two continents does Russia extend?
questions.explanation: Russia is a transcontinental country that extends across Europe (west of the Urals) and Asia (Siberia, to the east). It is also the largest country in the world by area.

questions.prompt: Which country was for a long time the most populated before being overtaken by India in 2023?
questions.explanation: China was the most populated country in the world for decades, until India overtook it in 2023. China's population has even begun to decline, a consequence of a low birth rate.

questions.prompt: How many oceans are recognized on Earth today?
questions.explanation: Today 5 oceans are recognized: Pacific, Atlantic, Indian, Southern and Arctic. The Southern Ocean, around Antarctica, is the most recently added to the list: it used to be said there were only four.
